summary: Joe Reis shares his low-tech, methodical approach to writing articles, which involves hand-written outlines on legal pads, zero drafts in Google Docs, and multiple editing passes. He emphasizes that writing is fundamentally about clarifying your own thinking, not just producing content.
key_points: Articles start with ideas that won't go away, followed by hand-written outlining on legal pads
Zero drafts are about getting words on the page regardless of quality
Editing is where real writing happens - multiple passes that get progressively finer
Writing and resting work together to clarify ideas
Writing is a selfish act of improving your own thinking
money_quotes: Editing is where the real writing starts.
The act of writing isn't only to blurt onto the page. It's a very personal and selfish act of improving your thinking.
Writing and resting work together to clarify your ideas. I find it's best to write, pause, process, and rewrite.
Outlining is super key for me, as my mind tends to be very chaotic and I've got a lot going on upstairs.
key_insight: Writing is less about producing content and more about using the iterative process of drafting, resting, and editing to sharpen and clarify your own thinking.
